raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 1.0, "R1.2": 0.3, "R1.3": 0.0, "R1.4": 0.3, "R1.5": 0.7, "R1.6": 0.3, "R1.7": 0.0, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.5", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.1": "Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' is definitionally a conceptual-reconstruction task — the artifact's three competing contribution claims (algorithm, regret bound, benchmark suite) are in structural tension, and reframing requires adjudicating which claim anchors the thesis and how the gap statement should be re-cast around it.", "R1.5": "Central: any successful conceptual reframing will propagate into a revised narrative arc — the current structure buries the self-described 'principal contribution' (the regret bound) in paragraph two and pivots abruptly to benchmark framing in paragraph three; reframing almost certainly demands reordering this arc."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should the reframing focus on reconceptualizing the paper's core contribution identity — choosing whether to position FairCPO primarily as a theory paper (regret bound), an algorithm paper (empirical NDCG gains), or an infrastructure paper (benchmark suite) — or on restructuring the narrative sequence so the existing three contributions are surfaced in a more effective order without changing which one is declared primary?"}

rationale.R1.1: Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' is definitionally a conceptual-reconstruction task — the artifact's three competing contribution claims (algorithm, regret bound, benchmark suite) are in structural tension, and reframing requires adjudicating which claim anchors the thesis and how the gap statement should be re-cast around it.
rationale.R1.5: Central: any successful conceptual reframing will propagate into a revised narrative arc — the current structure buries the self-described 'principal contribution' (the regret bound) in paragraph two and pivots abruptly to benchmark framing in paragraph three; reframing almost certainly demands reordering this arc.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the reframing focus on reconceptualizing the paper's core contribution identity — choosing whether to position FairCPO primarily as a theory paper (regret bound), an algorithm paper (empirical NDCG gains), or an infrastructure paper (benchmark suite) — or on restructuring the narrative sequence so the existing three contributions are surfaced in a more effective order without changing which one is declared primary?
